revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:石嘉波神社]]
{{Infobox religious building | Name = Ishikawa Shrine | image = File:石嘉波神社1.jpg | location = {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|qid=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Kunigami District, Okinawa|en|Kunigami District, Okinawa|qid=Q1330132|lt=Kunigami District|1=Kunigami District, Okinawa}}{{ill|Motobu, Okinawa|en|Motobu, Okinawa|qid=Q1205928|lt=Motobu|1=Motobu, Okinawa}} Sesoko 2261 | Latitude degrees = 26 | Latitude minutes = 38 | Latitude seconds = 47.2 | Longitude degrees = 127 | Longitude minutes = 52 | Longitude seconds = 8.7 | deity = Nedokoro Hi no Kami | Main object of worship = | Shrine Rank = | Founded = [[1928]] (Shōwa 3), July 28 ([[Lunar calendar|Lunisolar]] June 12) | Main hall = | Other names = [[Utaki (Okinawa)|Utaki]] Takisan | Notable festival = Uukui (Ufuyumi) | Rituals = | Map = Japan Okinawa | location = {{ill|Motobu|qid=Q1205928|1=Motobu, Okinawa}} | coordinates = {{Coord|26.64256111111111|N|127.87096111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Ishikawa Shrine''' (石嘉波神社, Ishikawa Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located on {{ill|Sesoko Island|en|Sesoko Island|qid=Q137962|lt=Sesoko Island|1=Sesoko Island}}, {{ill|Motobu, Okinawa|en|Motobu, Okinawa|qid=Q1205928|lt=Motobu, Okinawa|1=Motobu, Okinawa}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|qid=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}. The shrine refers to a slightly elevated forest area southeast of the Ishikawa settlement (dialect: Icchafa). The area is also known as '''{{ill|Utaki Takisan|ja|御嶽タキサン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utaki Takisan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''<ref name="Document1"></ref>. The shrine also includes {{ill|Nedokoro|ja|根所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nedokoro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Kami Asagi|ja|神アサギ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Asagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミアシャギ}}.
== Overview == The Ishikawa settlement (dialect: Icchafa) is southeast of a slightly elevated forest, which is the site of Ishikawa Shrine. Within the forest is a concrete-built {{ill|Hokora|en|Hokora|qid=Q1442984|lt=Hokora|1=Hokora}}, built on July 28, 1928, with a torii standing at the western entrance<ref name="Document1">{{Cite book |author=Motobu Town Board of Education |title=Cultural Properties of Motobu Town |volume=Vol. 5 |publisher=Motobu Town |date=1981-03-31 |ncid=BA74524293 |page=28}}</ref>.
Although it is called a shrine, the forest area also includes Nedokoro and the Kami Asagi (details below).
== History == Ishikawa Village has existed since the establishment of {{ill|Inoha Magiri|ja|伊野波間切|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoha Magiri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and was originally located adjacent to Kenken Village. However, due to the forest policies of {{ill|Sai On|en|Sai On|qid=Q851766|lt=Sai On|1=Sai On}}, during the reign of {{ill|Shō Kei|en|Shō Kei|qid=Q1041401|lt=King Shō Kei|1=Shō Kei}} in {{ill|1736|en|1736|qid=Q6989|lt=1736|1=1736}} ({{ill|Qianlong|ja|乾隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Qianlong|qq=}} 1), the village was relocated to the eastern side of Sesoko Island<ref>"{{ill|Kyūyō|en|Kyūyō|qid=Q3386686|lt=Kyūyō|1=Kyūyō}}" Volume 13: "Ishikawa Village Relocated to Sesoko Island"</ref>. Ishikawa Shrine was then created by the villagers as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.town.motobu.okinawa.jp/application/files/3515/8503/3558/HP_.pdf |title=Sesoko Island GUIDE MAP |website=www.town.motobu.okinawa.jp |accessdate=2023-09-08}}</ref>.
The village continued as Ishikawa Village, but in {{ill|1903|en|1903|qid=Q2045|lt=1903|1=1903}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 36), by Prefectural Order No. 36, Ishikawa Village was merged with Sesoko Village, forming a single village. Then, in {{ill|1908|en|1908|qid=Q2056|lt=1908|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), Sesoko Village of Motobu Magiri was renamed Motobu Village Sesoko, and in {{ill|1940|en|1940|qid=Q18633|lt=1940|1=1940}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 15), it became Motobu Town Sesoko<ref name="Document1"></ref>.
The Utaki Takisan of Ishikawa Village continued as the ancestral Utaki of the old Ishikawa settlement, and its {{ill|Saishi|ja|祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q132241}} has remained separate from that of Sesoko Village<ref name="Document1"></ref>. On the 20th day of the old seventh month, the "Uukui (Ufuyumi)" ritual is held, expressing gratitude for bountiful harvests and fisheries, praying for the villagers' health and prosperity<ref>{{Cite book |author=Yoshiaki Nakata |title=Shinugu of Motobu |publisher=Okinawa Studies Institute |series=Okinawa Studies Series 4 |date=2003-10-16 |id={{NDLSearch|R100000001-I471108382667}} |pages=57-58}}</ref>.
The "{{ill|Ryūkyū-koku yurai-ki|en|Ryūkyū-koku yurai-ki|qid=Q10992219|lt=Ryūkyū-koku yurai-ki|1=Ryūkyū-koku yurai-ki}}" (1713) mentions "Ishikawa Village, Maeno Utaki, deity Matsuno Wakkakasa no Oibe, Yonefusaki Utaki, deity Kanematsu Tsukasa no Oibe, Arasaki Utaki, deity Ishino Oibe," but these cannot be specifically identified<ref name="Document1"></ref>.
== Nedokoro == [[File:石嘉波神社1.jpg|thumb|Nedokoro]]
Nedokoro (dialect: Nīdukūru) is located about 30 meters north of Takisan. It is a {{ill|Bargeboard|en|Bargeboard|qid=Q4881511|lt=Hafu-style|1=Bargeboard}} concrete structure, measuring 2 meters in width, 1.6 meters in depth, and 2 meters in height. The enshrined deity is Nedokoro Hi no Kami<ref name="Document1"></ref>.
== Kami Asagi == [[File:Ishika shrine asagi.jpg|thumb|Kami Asagi]]
The Kami Asagi is a place to welcome and feast the deities.
At Ishikawa Shrine, the Kami Asagi is located on a slightly lower level west of Nedokoro. It is built with concrete pillars and a cement-tiled roof<ref name="Document1"></ref>.
== Notes == ; References {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == *Motobu Town Board of Education (1981). "Cultural Properties of Motobu Town Vol. 5." *Nakata, Yoshiaki (2003). "Shinugu of Motobu." Okinawa Studies Institute. *[https://www.town.motobu.okinawa.jp/application/files/3515/8503/3558/HP_.pdf Sesoko Island GUIDE MAP] (Retrieved 2023-09-08).
== See Also == *{{ill|Sesoko Island|en|Sesoko Island|qid=Q137962|lt=Sesoko Island|1=Sesoko Island}} *{{ill|Motobu, Okinawa|en|Motobu, Okinawa|qid=Q1205928|lt=Motobu, Okinawa|1=Motobu, Okinawa}} *{{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} *{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} *{{ill|Ontake|en|Ontake|qid=Q11071176|lt=Utaki|1=Ontake}}
